methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron. The valuation universe comprises 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Zephyr Fusion. The broader nuclear energy sector encompasses 4 observable same-sector companies, though the pre-seed stage positioning creates a narrow reference set.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Deep Atomic. The analysis identified zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers meeting minimum thresholds. A defensible peer-median approach requires at least 3 datapoints within filtered categories; the available 4 same-sector peers do not satisfy these narrow criteria. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive dispersion and fail to reflect nuclear energy–specific capital structures and risk profiles.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without additional data on Deep Atomic's operational milestones, capital requirements, or IP position, and without access to specific valuation and financing terms from the identified private peer set. The scarcity of pre-seed nuclear energy transactions limits comparable transaction analysis. Valuation at this stage will depend primarily on bespoke assessment of founder track record, technology differentiation, and regulatory pathway clarity rather than multiples-based inference.
